question: A projectile is fired at an angle θ with the horizontal. At the highest point of its trajectory, its speed is half of its initial speed. The angle θ is:
The equation of a projectile is y = √3x - gx²/2. The angle of projection is:
A boat can travel with a speed of 5 km/h in still water. If the speed of the river is 3 km/h, the shortest time to cross a 1 km wide river is:
For a projectile, the ratio of maximum height to the square of time of flight is:
A particle is moving in a circle of radius R with constant speed v. The magnitude of change in velocity when it covers an angle of 60° is:
A man can swim in still water at 4 km/h. He wants to cross a river flowing at 2 km/h. At what angle with the river flow should he swim to reach the point exactly opposite?
The horizontal range and maximum height of a projectile are equal. The angle of projection is:
A stone is thrown at an angle of 45° to the horizontal with kinetic energy K. The kinetic energy at the highest point is:
If R is the range of a projectile on a horizontal plane and H is the maximum height, then the maximum range for the same speed of projection is:
Rain is falling vertically with a speed of 30 m/s. A man rides a bicycle with a speed of 10 m/s in the north to south direction. What is the direction in which he should hold his umbrella?
A particle starts from origin at t=0 with velocity 5i m/s and moves in x-y plane under a force such that it has acceleration (3i + 2j) m/s². The y-coordinate when x-coordinate is 84m is:
Two projectiles are thrown with the same speed at angles (45-θ) and (45+θ). The ratio of their horizontal ranges is:
A stone tied to the end of a string 1m long is whirled in a horizontal circle with a constant speed. If the stone makes 22 revolutions in 44 seconds, the magnitude of acceleration is:
A projectile is thrown with speed u at an angle θ. The radius of curvature of its path at the highest point is:
The position vector of a particle is r = (acosωt)i + (asinωt)j. The velocity of the particle is:
A body is projected with a velocity of 30 m/s at an angle of 30° with the vertical. Its horizontal range is (g=10):
A particle moves in x-y plane with velocity v = ai + bxj. If it passes through origin, the equation of its path is:
The maximum range of a gun on horizontal terrain is 16 km. If g = 10 m/s², the muzzle velocity of a shell must be:
In a uniform circular motion, the acceleration vector is:

options.A: Constant in magnitude but variable in direction
options.B: Constant in direction but variable in magnitude

question: A bullet is dropped from the same height at the same time as another bullet is fired horizontally. They will hit the ground:

Two particles are projected with same speed but different angles θ₁ and θ₂. If their horizontal ranges are same, then:
A particle moves in a plane with constant acceleration 'a' in a direction different from the initial velocity 'v'. The path of the particle is a:
A person can throw a ball to a maximum horizontal distance of 100m. How high can he throw the same ball vertically?
If the instantaneous velocity of a particle is v = (3i + 4j) m/s and its constant acceleration is a = (4i + 3j) m/s², its speed after 1 second is:
The time of flight of a projectile is 10s and its range is 500m. The maximum height reached by it is (g=10):
A cyclist turns around a curve at 15 miles/hour. If he turns at double the speed, the tendency to overturn is:
A ball is projected from a tower of height H with velocity u horizontally. The velocity with which it hits the ground is:
